commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6d14d7f466801b100e53027931d3842f32042de6)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a Feishu merge_forward interactive-card text extractor and tests so forwarded cards parse nested card text instead of returning `[interactive]`.

PR surface: Source +38, Tests +66. Total +104 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main fetches merge_forward details into `parseMergeForwardContent`, whose formatter lacks an `interactive` case and returns `[interactive]`. I did not run a live Feishu tenant reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This external PR needs contributor or maintainer revision plus after-fix Feishu proof; ClawSweeper should not auto-repair while the real-setup proof gate remains unsatisfied.

target.title: fix(feishu): parse interactive card text in merge_forward sub-messages
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a Feishu bot receives a **merge_forward** message, OpenClaw fetches the full
content via `im.message.get` and expands each sub-message through
`formatSubMessageContent` (`extensions/feishu/src/bot-content.ts`). That function
had no case for `interactive` (card) sub-messages, so any forwarded card — tables,
rich layouts, columns — fell through to the `default` branch and collapsed to the
bare placeholder `[interactive]`, losing all of its text.

This is the exact symptom in #77909:

```
feishu[default]: Feishu[default] DM from ou_xxx: [Merged and Forwarded Messages] - [interactive]
```

The API returns the card body, but the parser discards it.

## Why This Change Was Made

Feishu interactive cards nest their display text under `content` fields at
arbitrary depth — header `title.content`, `{tag:"markdown", content}`, and
`div`/column `text.content`. The plugin's own card builders already follow this
shape (`presentation-card.ts`, `outbound.ts`, `card-ux-approval.ts`). The fix adds
an `interactive` case that walks the card and collects those `content` strings, so
forwarded cards keep their text. If a card genuinely has no text (e.g. image-only),
it falls back to a readable `[Interactive Card]` placeholder instead of `[interactive]`.

Scope is intentionally narrow: only the new `interactive` branch and a small
recursive text collector are added; no other sub-message type changes.

## User Impact

Forwarding a Feishu card (including tables) to the bot now preserves the original
text content instead of dropping it. No config or API changes.

## Evidence

AI-assisted implementation; proof generated by driving the **real** exported
`parseMergeForwardContent` (not a mock) on a realistic `im.message.get` payload for
a merge_forward of one interactive card (header + markdown + two-column table-like
layout), before vs after the fix.

**Before (baseline `upstream/main`)** — reproduces the reporter's production symptom:

```
---PARSE OUTPUT START---
[Merged and Forwarded Messages]
- [interactive]
---PARSE OUTPUT END---
```

**After (this PR):**

```
---PARSE OUTPUT START---
[Merged and Forwarded Messages]
- Q2 Sales Report
**Region**: APAC  |  **Total**: 1,240
Q1: 540
Q2: 700
---PARSE OUTPUT END---
```

Unit tests (`extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts`), exercising the real
`parseMergeForwardContent`:

- `extracts text from interactive card sub-messages instead of dropping to a placeholder`
- `keeps a placeholder when an interactive card carries no text`

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts -t "interactive card"
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed | 87 skipped (89)
```

Full `extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts` suite green; oxlint + oxfmt clean.

## Merge risk

Low / additive. Only adds a new `interactive` switch arm in
`formatSubMessageContent`, reached solely by merge_forward sub-message expansion;
behavior for every other message type is unchanged. The card walker only reads
`content` strings, so it cannot throw on unexpected card shapes (returns `""` →
falls back to `[Interactive Card]`).

Closes #77909
